title: Module 4: Explainability & Audit Trail Schema
description: Machine-readable audit framework for evaluating AI explainability and audit trail adequacy meeting PCAOB evidence standards

evaluation_criteria.criterion_name: AI Decision Explainability for External Auditors
evaluation_criteria.description: AI-driven accounting decisions can be understood and challenged by auditors per PCAOB AS 1105
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review SHAP values, LIME explanations, model cards; test whether explanations enable decision traceability
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Explanations enable traceability, model cards meet ISO 42001 standards, PCAOB evidence standards met

evidence_required.sample_criteria: Representative of decision types (high/low confidence, different contract values, different customer segments)
evidence_required.auditor_interpretability_test: Independent auditor can trace explanation to source data and understand decision logic

components.calculation: Required sections present / Total required sections

components.assessment: Can auditors trace decisions to source data?
Do explanations constitute sufficient appropriate audit evidence?
components.sufficiency: Adequate quantity of explanations covering transaction population
components.appropriateness: Explanations are relevant and reliable for audit purposes

severity_mapping.justification: Inadequate explainability prevents auditors from evaluating AI-driven accounting estimates per PCAOB AS 2501, creating audit scope limitation

audit_opinion_impact.financial_statement_audit: Likely qualified opinion or disclaimer if AI systems material and unexplainable
audit_opinion_impact.icfr_audit: Adverse opinion possible - controls cannot be understood/tested

regulatory_mapping.pcaob_as_1105: Insufficient audit evidence - explanations do not enable auditor to assess reliability
regulatory_mapping.pcaob_as_2501: Cannot evaluate reasonableness of AI-generated accounting estimates
regulatory_mapping.eu_ai_act: Article 13 violation - transparency requirements not met

evaluation_criteria.description: Complete audit trail capturing all AI decisions, model versions, and data lineage
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review audit trail logs, test traceability from financial statement back to AI decision and source data
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: All AI decisions logged with timestamps, model versions, inputs, outputs; retention meets regulatory requirements

evidence_required.required_mapping: Source data → AI model → Financial statement line item
evidence_required.traceability_test: Auditor can trace any financial statement amount back to AI decision and source data

components.calculation: AI decisions logged / Total AI decisions made
Required fields populated / Total required fields

components.test: Sample 25 financial statement amounts; verify traceable to AI decision and source data

severity_mapping.justification: Incomplete audit trail prevents verification of financial statement accuracy and violates record retention requirements

classification_logic.below_0.5: Material Weakness - extensive gaps prevent audit
classification_logic.0.5_to_0.8: Significant Deficiency - some gaps exist but partial audit possible

regulatory_mapping.pcaob_as_1105: Audit evidence inadequate - cannot verify AI decisions

evaluation_criteria.criterion_name: Model Version Control and Change Documentation
evaluation_criteria.description: All AI model changes documented with impact assessment and audit trail
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review model version history, change logs, impact assessments, approval records
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: All model updates documented, impact assessments completed, changes approved by control owners

evidence_required.approval_requirement: Finance control owner approval for production deployments

scoring.calculation: Model changes with complete documentation / Total model changes

severity_mapping.justification: Undocumented model changes prevent auditors from assessing impact on financial reporting controls

aggregate_scoring.note: If E4.1 or E4.2 < 0.85, overall module fails - explainability and audit trail are non-negotiable for financial audits

interdependency_triggers.trigger_condition: E4.1 or E4.2 score < 0.7 (inadequate explainability or audit trail)
interdependency_triggers.action: Flag all other module findings as 'Lower Confidence' since lack of explainability prevents full validation
interdependency_triggers.escalation: Orchestration layer escalates to human auditors as potential scope limitation per PCAOB AS 1105 (insufficient audit evidence)

interdependency_triggers.action: Perform detailed explainability review to determine if drift broke explanation validity
investigation_questions: Are SHAP explanations still valid given drift?
Is drift concept drift (explanations meaningful) or model breakdown (explanations invalid)?
Can auditors still rely on explanations for PCAOB AS 1105 compliance?
